title: Ranunculus hilarans
taxonomy: Ranunculus hilarans is a species within the Ranunculaceae family. This family is commonly known as the buttercup or crowfoot family. Ranunculaceae comprises around 1,900 to 2,100 species, which are mostly found in the Northern Hemisphere. The family is divided into about 44 genera, with Ranunculus being one of the largest and most diverse. Ranunculus hilarans is further classified within the genus Ranunculus, which includes a wide range of flowering plants.

intro: Ranunculus hilarans, also known as the Golden Buttercup, is a flowering plant species native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Ranunculus hilarans include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are prepared by steeping the dried flowers, leaves, or roots in hot water or a solvent.
